REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, OR ANY PORTIONS THEREOF, IS IMPROPER, FALSE OR IRRELEVANT OR NOT DRAWN IN CONFORMITY WITH THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA. PARAGRAPHS 2-8 OF CHAU DECLARATION ARE STRICKEN. THIS IS THE SECOND TIME IS 3 MONTHS COUNSEL SUBMITS, WITHOUT ANY CITATION TO AUTHORITY "EVIDENCE" IN CONJUNCTION WITH A PLEADING CHALLENGE. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANTS OPPOSITIONS HAVE BEEN FILED LATE DUE TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS MISTAKEN IDENTIFICATION OF THE PARTIES FILING THE PAPERS. THIS IS NOT THE FIRST TIME THE TIMELY SUBMITTED FILINGS ARE REJECTED FOR MISTAKES OF THIS KIND (WRONG PARTY NAME; WRONG PARTY IDENTIFICATION; WRONG PLEADINGS DESIGNATION, ETC). Wendy Chau, Law Office of Wendy Chau, 415-539-6116, in-person appearance for plaintiff and cross-defendant
Walter Lin, Steiner Law Office, 415-931-4207, via zoom for defendant and cross complainant

Mr. Lin contests to the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TROY SNOWDEN DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The Court has previously given the Cross-Complainant an opportunity to allege facts in support of the element of justifiable reliance on the Cross-Defendant's' misrepresentations. Cross-Complaint failed to so allege. Cross-Complainant did not allege any facts showing that the cross-complaint believed the representations or that such believe was reasonable. Instead, Cross-Complaint alleges facts showing that Cross-Complaint "would" or possibly did instead investigate the misrepresentation, i.e. did not believe or rely on them reasonably or otherwise instead Cross-Complaint proceeded to conduct discovery to rebut the misrepresentations.

Order signed. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Wendy Chau Esq., Law office of Wendy Chau, 415-539-6116, in-person appearance for plaintiff/cross defendant
Walter Lin Esq., Steiner Law Office, 415-931-4207, via zoom for defendant/cross complainant

Mr. Lin informs the Court that notice was improper. Ms Chau gives notice on Sep-27-24 which is a court holiday.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN, SUMMER SALIBA MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry its burden and show that any part of the Cross-Complaint is false, irrelevant or improper. The Court will not find that the Cross-Complaint was untimely filed as the Court records indicate that Cross-Complainant attempted to file the Cross-Complaint on June 25,2024. The Court notes that the delay could have been avoided had cross-complainant presented an order on the motion for leave to file a cross-complaint at the hearing. The order would have been executed on June 25, 2024 at the hearing and any delay would have been avoided. The Court further notes that Cross-Complainant failed to properly spell their name on the Cross-Complaint to match it with the other filings in this case "Steve" vs. "Steven." The appropriate spelling of the name would avoid any further delay. Declaration of Ms. Chau in Support of Motion to Strike is stricken except for paragraph 21.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reported with FTR. (D501)

Howard Olsen; Steiner Law Office; 415-931-4207; appearing as counsel to defendants S&S Hospitality Inc. and Steve Brown.

Both sides submit to the court's tentative decision. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS S&S HOSPITALITY INC. AND STEVE BROWN'S DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action for hostile work environment. Plaintiff alleges employment dates "from December 1st2014 through about February 21, 2019." Plaintiff fails to allege that a claim was filed with DFEH within one year of that date or that a right to sue letter was obtain during that time. Authorities cited by Plaintiff in opposition do not support Plaintiff's contention that no right to sue letter was required under the circumstance of this case or obtaining such letter on February 2, 2024 was timely.
